paths.name: react
paths.message: useActionState is a React 19 API and throws at runtime on React 18.3.1 (we're pinned to 18 via Next 14). Use `useFormState` + `useFormStatus` from `react-dom` instead. See ClientForm.tsx for the canonical SubmitButton pattern. Lift this rule when upgrading to React 19 + Next 15.

paths.name: react-dom
paths.message: useActionState was only ever exported from `react`, not `react-dom`. On React 18.3.1 use `useFormState` + `useFormStatus` from `react-dom`.
